sections.title: Scaffold Erection and Inspection Checklist
sections.html:
Inspection Frequency Note: Scaffolds must be inspected by a competent person prior to each work shift and after any occurrence which could affect its structural integrity (e.g., severe weather, impact). (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3) and WAC 296-869-200(2))

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

Corrective Action Required

1

Base plates and mudsills are level and sound (per 29 CFR 1926.451(c)(2))

☐

☐

☐

2

Scaffold is plumb, level, and square (per 29 CFR 1926.451(d)(3))

☐

☐

☐

3

All components are in good condition and free from damage (per 29 CFR 1926.451(b)(1))

☐

☐

☐

4

Guardrails are installed on all open sides and ends of platforms (per 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(4))

☐

☐

☐

5

Midrails are installed approximately halfway between the toprail and the platform surface (per 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(4)(ii))

☐

☐

☐

6

Toeboards are installed on all open sides and ends of platforms (per 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(4)(iii))

☐

☐

☐

7

Platforms are fully planked or decked between the front uprights and the guardrail supports (per 29 CFR 1926.451(b)(1))

☐

☐

☐

8

Platforms are secured to prevent displacement (per 29 CFR 1926.451(b)(2))

☐

☐

☐

9

Access to scaffold is safe and appropriate (ladders, ramps, etc.) (per 29 CFR 1926.451(e))

☐

☐

☐

10

Scaffold is tied, guyed, or braced as required (per 29 CFR 1926.451(c)(1))

☐

☐

☐

11

Competent person has inspected scaffold before each shift (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(3))

☐

☐

☐

12

Fall protection is provided for employees working on scaffolds 10 feet or more above a lower level (per 29 CFR 1926.451(g)(1))

☐

☐

☐

13

Scaffold is not overloaded (per 29 CFR 1926.451(a)(1))

☐

☐

☐

14

Electrical hazards are identified and controlled (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(6))

☐

☐

☐

15

Overhead protection is provided where necessary (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(7))

☐

☐

☐

16

Wheels and casters are locked on mobile scaffolds (per 29 CFR 1926.452(w)(2))

☐

☐

☐

17

Scaffold is erected, dismantled, or altered only under the supervision of a competent person (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(1))

☐

☐

☐

18

Training has been provided to all employees working on scaffolds (per 29 CFR 1926.454)

☐

☐

☐

19

Clearances from power lines are maintained (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(6))

☐

☐

☐

20

Debris chutes or other means are used to remove debris (per 29 CFR 1926.451(f)(8))

☐

☐

☐
